reasoning: The total remaining export capacity for Russian gas to the EU in 2025 is physically capped far below the 15 bcm/quarter (60 bcm/year) threshold. With the closure of the Ukraine transit route in January 2025 (which historically supplied ~15 bcm/year), Russia is left with only TurkStream (~15.75 bcm/year throughput capacity for Europe) and LNG exports (historically ~18-20 bcm/year to the EU). Running both at 100% maximum capacity equates to roughly 34 bcm/year, or an average of ~8.5 bcm per quarter. Even accommodating hypothetical seasonal spikes in LNG, it is structurally impossible for imports to nearly double and reach 15 bcm in a single quarter of 2025. In light of the ongoing REPowerEU phasedown and recent data indicating 2024 imports were exceptionally low, there is no credible pathway to resolving "Yes." The consensus crowd forecast of 1% accurately reflects this physical and political impossibility. I assign the minimum allowable probability of 2%.
